title: Direct Routing and Bounded Worker Pools · Goroutine Forensics
summary: An incident packet contains a plausible a capacity-aware Go work distribution service with one stale generation, blocked notification, goroutine leak, acknowledgement, route, or effect defect; locate cause before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a capacity-aware Go work distribution service and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defens…
objectives: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Design direct exchange bindings that keep producer intent stable while consumer queues evolve; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Use one queue with multiple consumers for load distribution and state that each delivery goes to one active consumer at a time; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Default Exchange Coupling
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go messaging team handles case 9349 during stream-versus-queue architecture board. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Explain the default exchange queue-name binding and choose it only when direct queue-name coupling is intentional; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Inspect the Default Exchange Coupling trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Worker Effect Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-go-worker-effect-evidence-qglnl6" that uses case 1793 to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqp091-go, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, race, fuzz, leak, amqp091-go 1.12.0, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production gates.

At RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Worker Effect Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug Go messaging team handles case 2682 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine direct routing, worker capacity, and acknowledgement-after-effect evidence; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Acknowledge only after a durable effect or safely repeatable handoff and retain message identity, attempt, result, and failure evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Worker Effect Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A worker should acknowledge before starting slow work to prevent duplicates. In Go, one goroutine per delivery is not a capacity or fairness policy.
